ref: p111, Birdcage Walk. Putative: Landed on Guards Chapel, see Jones 'Most Secret War' p.424

"position": "Howie Street at junction with Radstock Street"
"notes": "V1 demolished 10 houses and badly damaged a further 40"

"position": "Brixton Road Cambewell New Road junction SE Quad"
"notes": "The V1 struck Brixton Road and Camberwell New Road. A range of buildings used as workrooms
column10: office and stores suffered severe damage"

"position": "The junction of the High St and Gwynne Road (around 110 High St)"
"notes": "V1 Destroyed 110 High St and also damaged a van and some timber"

"position": "Petworth Street East side junction with Bolan Street"
"notes": "The V1 demolished 6 houses and damaged 20 others"

"position": "Paradise Road Clarence walk junction SW quad"
"notes": "The V1 fell in Paradise Road. Stockwell Methodist Church Hall was damaged."
"1 of 2 V1's in Gauden Road. I cannot ascertain which date matches which location. One fell at the Station end
column10: the other on the North side by Broomfield Road junction."

"notes": "2nd of 2 V1's in Gauden Road. I cannot ascertain which date matches with location. One fell at the Station end
column10: the other on the North side by Broomfield Road junction."

"notes": "The V1 hit Clapham Road opposite Clapham North Tube. 6 houses were demolished and 20 houses and a Church severely damaged. The area has been re-developed as a housing estate and the church re-built"
"The V1 fell in Studley Road. 10 houses were demolished and 30 severely damaged."
"The V1 fell in Paradise Road and hit a gasometer at the South Metropolitan gas company"
"V1 in Stockwell Park Road by L orn Road."
"The V1 hit Manor house road. 20 houses demolished and 30 damaged."

"position": "Rumsey road between Benedict and Speenham Road"
"notes": "The V1 struck in Rumsey Road. 12 houses were demolished and 40 damaged."
"V1 in Barrington Road. No casualties reported. 2 houses demolished and 40 damaged an ews brick and steel dam damaged by blast."
"The V1 hit Pathos Road and Lothian Street.8 houses were demolished and 30 badly damaged."

"position": "Honeywell Road Between Honeywell and Broomwood Road : Norhcross road junction"
"notes": "V1 destroyed 8 houses and badly damaged a further 20"
"V1 on open ground on Clapham Common. Slight damage to surrounding property."
"V1 fell on open ground on Clapham Common
"V1 fell on Clapham Common near Mount Pond. No damage is reported"
"The V1 fell on the South side of Clapham Common. Some damage to surrounding property"

"position": "Lesser Avenue Position between Lessar and Cautley avenues half way up"
"notes": "The V1 struck Lesser Avenue and demolished 20 houses and caused severe damage to 20 others. There was a small fire in the debris."
"The V1 fell in Cavendish Road and demolished 6 houses and caused severe damage to 20 others. The area has been re-developed with a housing estate"
"The 2nd of 2 V1's that fell in this area. Very little details were recorded. The area has been totally re-developed as a housing estate."
"One of 2 V1's that fell in this area. 12 houses were demolished in Kepler Road
column10: 20 badly damaged in Kepler Road and 12 in Solon New Road. The area has been totally re-developed as a housing estate"

"notes": "The V1 fell at the junction of Kings Avenue and Clarence Road. It demolished 12 flats and 40 houses and also caused much damage to Queenswood court. This building has tell tale metal window frames which were installed as replacements."

"This very serious incident occurred when the V1 struck a row of shops/buildings opposite the Town Hall in Acre Lane. One of the buildings was being used as a rest centre for people who had previously been forced out of their homes by Flying bombs. As well as the rest centre 10 houses and shops were demolished and 20 houses damaged. Lambeth Town Hall
column11: clearly stood up well to the blast and no structural damage can be detected. However careful inspection will reveal that the Windows frames down the side of the Acre Lane side of the town hall are in a slightly different style to elsewhere in the building and must have been completely blown out. On the other side of the road the sight of the rest centre and shops has been replaced with 1960's style new shops filling the gap."

"notes": "The V1 struck between Baytree and Sudbourne Road. 20 houses were demolished and 40 very badly damaged. The area has been re-developed with post war flats."

"position": "Trelawn Road Half way up South side"
"notes": "The V1 hit The South side of Trelawn road.11 houses were demolished and 40 badly damaged in both Trelawn and Morval Roads. The gap in the line of housing can still be seen. In Morval road the in-filling in now being rebuilt for a second time since the war time demolition"

"The V1 struck at the junction of Water Lane And St Matthew Road. 10 houses were demolished and 30 damaged. The area today has been totally re-developed so it is not possible to ascertain the impact site"
"This V1 fell in Lyham Road between the Wall of Brixton prison and Dumbarton Road. 8 houses were demolished and 30 badly damaged."
"The V1 fell at the South East end of Beechdale Road totally demolishing 12 houses and badly damaging 20. A further 12 were damaged in Endymion road."
"The V1 divested the area around the Effra Parad/Dalberg Road junction. 12 houses were demolished and 40 badly damaged. The area today has been totally re-developed with flats."
"The V1 fell in Brockwell Park. No casualties were reported by the Fire Brigade an Open air Theatre and 50 houses were damaged by the blast."
"21 people were injured by this V1 strike. It demolished 14 houses in Bellasis Avenue and badly damaged 50 in Bellasis Avenue
column10: Criefell Avenue and Thornton Avenue. The road has been totally re-developed with 60's housing"

"notes": "The V1 struck the Streatham Hill theatre on the Barhill road side. It was partly demolished and surrounding property was badly damaged. The Theatre was re-built and is currently in use as a bingo hall. The re-built flank wall clearly shows the scars of the V1"

"The V1 injured 24 people. 3 houses were demolished and 30 badly damaged in Montrell Road. 3 were demolished and 20 damaged in Tierney Road. A further 3 were demolished and 20 damaged in Streatham Place. Today post war housing fills the site but the tell tale signs of blanked off gable ends of once terraces housing is clear to see."

"position": "Streatham Hill/Opposite end of Wyatt Park Road"
"notes": "The V1 struck in the Streatham High Road opposite the end of Wyatt Park Road.A number of shops
column10: flats and private houses were badly damaged. Looking at the flats opposite the end of Wyatt Park Road you can see the main impact site of the V1 where a V shaped wedge has been taken from the building and then patched up with a slightly different coloured brickwork."

"notes": "The V1 impacted on the North side of Barcombe Avenue a short way up from Streatham High Road. 3 houses were totally demolished and a number badly damaged on both sides of the road. Post war housing can be seen on both side of the road here
column10: indicating the impact site and interrupting the line of the normal style of housing seen in the ABC roads in Streatham"

"position": "Downton Avenue West End North side North side towards Streatham High Road"
"notes": "This V1 fell on the North side of Downton Avenue a short way up from Streatham High Road. 10 houses were demolished and 17 severely damaged. Lesser damage across a wide area. The houses have been re-built to match undamaged counterparts. The only difference that can be detected is that the flank walls display the fact that they have been built using a red-brick common in the 40's
column10: the originals used London stock yellow bricks for the same walls."

"notes": "The V1 badly damaged the girls school at the Wavertree Road/Daysbrook Road junction. 10 house were demolished and 20 badly damaged in Daysbrook Road. 2 were demolished and 10 badly damaged in Wavertree Road. The damage area extended right down Daysbrook Road
column10: including number 2 slightly damaged where I lived for 6 years from 1990. The school has been re-built and carries a plaque which commemorates the event. The demolished houses in Daysbrook Road have been replaced with 60's housing
column11: further down the frontages of the remaining Victorian houses show signs of significant re-building."

"notes": "The V1 struck at the junction of Amesbury Road and Hillside Road. 10 houses were totally demolished and a large number badly damaged. The area around the impact site has been extensively re-developed with post war housing and shops"
"The V1 struck in Kingsmead road just short of the sharp bend. 2 houses were totally demolished and 40 others very badly damaged."

"The V1 fell between Christchurch and Lanercost Road a short way up from the junction with Hillside road. .. 6 houses were demolished and 20 very badly damaged. Today the line of large Victorian houses in Christchurch road is interrupted by post war housing development which extend back into Lanercost road."
"The V1 demolished 4 houses on Tulse Hill in the area of the post war St Martins Estate A further 30 were badly damaged. No casualties were reported by the Fire Brigade."
"The V1 fell in open space in Brockwell Park. No casualties are reported by the Fire Brigade but some damage to surrounding buildings is noted."
